---
title: "City of Boise, Idaho"
subtitle: "2000"
number: 0
image: /images/boise_ada_ID.jpg
image-alt: "Image of Boise, Idaho"
categories: ["Integrated Solid Waste Planning", "Service Procurement"]
---
{{< include _return-to-pf.qmd >}}

::: {.article}

# Solid Waste Management Plan

Mr. Bell was a sub-consultant who assisted the City of Boise with the
development of Idaho's first SWMP, helping the City form a Solid Waste
Advisory Team (SWAT) and reach consensus on waste management priorities.
Recommendations included automated curbside collection, graduated rates
(pay-as-you-throw), formal disposal contracts with Ada County including a shift
to weight-based tip fees instead of volume-based fees, and phased-in yard waste
collection and composting. Information materials were presented at a dozen
workshops and to City staff, the SWAT, Public Works Commission, City Council
and Mayor to inform them of plan progress on technical issues and to solicit
their input on the project.

# Collection Contract Negotiation

Bell & Associates assisted the City with the cost and rate impacts of various
collection alternatives during the contract negotiations with Allied Waste of
Boise. Alternatives that were reviewed and implemented during this process
included residential cart recycling, fully automated collection, and
replacement of the diesel powered collection fleet with CNG trucks.

TODO: Contents
[By MATTHEW HARRISON SMITH - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0](https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=29928444)
:::
